title: viola silvatica subsp. insularis
intro: Viola silvatica subsp. insularis, also known as the island violet or wild violet, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the violet family, characterized by its distinctive purple flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The medicinal properties of Viola silvatica subsp. insularis include its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, Viola silvatica subsp. insularis is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used topically in creams and salves to promote wound healing and skin health.
